Memorial Day oversize/overweight freight restrictions began at noon May 22 through May 26 in most states, halting all flatbed OS/OW moves during peak flatbed season (flatbed spot up 27% YoY)

Maverick Transportation announced +4 cents/mile for all flatbed OTR drivers effective May 31, pushing starting pay to 59 cents/mile ($73K-$80K/year) — signal of accelerating carrier wage inflation ahead of summer
LMI Transportation Prices hit 95.0 in May 2026 (near-record high) while Transportation Capacity fell 40% since January, driven by Strait of Hormuz fuel disruption and carrier exit from the market
Pallet raised $27M for workflow AI targeting freight brokers, 3PLs, carriers, and shippers with agentic tools for scheduling, document processing, and shipment coordination below TMS level
Flexport launched latest tech rollout amid global freight chaos, continuing tariff compliance tool push and IPO positioning with 30% revenue growth target for 2026
Drewry WCI May 21: Shanghai-LA at $3,385/FEU (+1% WoW); ONE announced $2,000 PSS effective June 1 as transpacific carriers push for rate increases ahead of peak season

Robinson Fresh (C.H. Robinson) opened $33M, 142,600 sq ft cold-chain hub in Pharr TX on May 23, 4.5 miles from the Pharr-Reynosa International Bridge — the highest-volume US-Mexico produce crossing — directly competing in Uber Freight's strategic cross-border reefer corridor

Iran formalized permanent Strait of Hormuz toll system in talks with Oman via the Persian Gulf Strait Authority, imposing $1M+ tolls per vessel and holding diesel at $5.38/gal with bunker fuel at $800/MT since February 28 blockade began
FedEx Freight when-issued trading (FDXF WI) begins May 27 ahead of NYSE June 1 debut as independent $9B LTL carrier with 4-6% revenue growth targets and 12% OR goal under standalone investor pressure

Legal analysis confirms Supreme Court's Montgomery ruling extends to shippers — enterprise shippers who select brokers or carriers without documented vetting now face analogous state-law negligent hiring exposure, insurers requiring documented procedures from all chain participants

Deep Current survey found 74% of freight professionals make 50+ operational decisions per day and 83% operate in reactive mode despite widespread AI adoption due to fragmentation across 5+ daily systems

Waabi and Volvo unveiled production-ready VNL Autonomous truck powered by NVIDIA DRIVE AGX Thor/Hyperion 10 architecture at ACT Expo May 5, manufactured at Volvo's New River Valley VA plant, targeting Uber Freight load corridors driver-out by year-end 2026

Uber launched Autonomous Solutions suite February 23 2026 offering infrastructure, UX, and fleet operations as a service to AV partners including VW partnership for LA robotaxi late 2026, positioning Uber network as load-supply backbone for multiple AV stacks

DHL Supply Chain scaled HappyRobot AI agents across hundreds of thousands of emails and millions of voice minutes annually for appointment scheduling, driver follow-up, and warehouse coordination at global enterprise scale

Freightos May 26 weekly update: transpacific spot rates up 10%+ WoW to ~$2,800/FEU West Coast and ~$4,300/FEU East Coast as Amazon Prime Day moves to June, ONE announcing $2,000/FEU peak season surcharge effective June 1

C.H. Robinson CFO downplayed Montgomery insurance impact (insurance <0.5% gross revenue, locked in for 2026), positioned CHR as industry consolidator with $6 EPS guidance backed by 30 AI agents completing 100% rate quotes in 32 seconds

FedEx Freight FDXF when-issued trading begins May 27 ahead of NYSE June 1 debut as independent $9B LTL carrier; UBS flags sum-of-parts valuation upside from the separation under standalone investor pressure
CMA CGM Q1 2026 revenue $13.2B (flat YoY), EBITDA -31.6% to $2.1B on lower maritime rates, but logistics/terminal other activities jumped +59% YoY to $1.3B as volumes grew 1.5% to 5.9M TEU

FreightWaves 2026 Fraud Fighters Award winners announced: Highway (527K+ fraudulent emails blocked in Q1), Arrive Logistics (0.0004% theft ratio), MacroPoint, GenLogs, MyCarrierPortal, and Samsara recognized

Amazon Prime Day moved to June 2026 with FBA inbound deadline May 27, driving early ocean peak season surcharges of $2,000/FEU and expected June domestic freight demand conversion surge from ocean-to-inland container flows

IMO adopted first global Maritime Autonomous Surface Ships (MASS) Code on May 22, effective July 1, 2026 as non-mandatory voluntary framework covering four degrees of autonomy, on track for mandatory status by 2028

Trump announced a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is 'largely negotiated,' a 60-day ceasefire framework that would clear mines and restore toll-free passage, potentially cutting diesel $1-2/gal from current $5.38/gal
FedEx Freight FDXF WI when-issued trading began May 27 on NYSE ahead of full June 1 debut as independent $9B LTL carrier with public price discovery creating enterprise shipper evaluation window
USMCA Joint Review commences July 1 with Trump expected to seek rules-of-origin tightening and Mexico concessions threatening nearshoring supply chains driving Mexico's 15.5% share of US imports
SAFER Transport Act (HR 8267) advancing in Congress to phase out MC numbers in 5 years, criminalize carrier registration fraud, and establish Freight Fraud and Theft Advisory Committee

CARB settled with Volvo Group North America for $197M over excess diesel emissions from 10,000+ engines (2010-2016 MY), with $108M to California clean-fleet projects and Q2 2026 charge boosting AV/EV fleet economics
AIT Worldwide Logistics majority stake acquired by Greenbriar Equity Group in February 2026 in one of the largest-ever private freight forwarding deals with close expected Q4 2026

Post-Memorial Day freight market normalization: dry van ~$2.47/mile (down from $2.84 Roadcheck peak), OTRI declining from 13.4% national high, Amazon FBA standard Prime Day deadline May 27 driving last-mile demand

C.H. Robinson CFO confirmed Montgomery insurance impact less than 0.5% gross revenue locked for 2026, raised OI guidance to $965M-$1.04B, positioned CHR as post-SCOTUS industry consolidator with 30 AI agents at 32-second rate quotes
Flexport opened Indonesia office as 6th country expansion in 2026, targeting 30% revenue growth and IPO readiness while pursuing market share from DHL and CHR in international freight forwarding

Iran state TV reported 14-point draft framework with U.S. would reopen Strait of Hormuz within one month, sending WTI below $89/barrel on May 27 before the White House denied the report as a fabrication
FedEx Freight Holding (FDXF) announced for S&P 500 inclusion effective June 2 replacing EPAM and Dow Jones Transportation Average June 1 replacing American Airlines, when-issued trading began May 27
USTR Deputy Ambassador Jeff Goettman leads first bilateral USMCA negotiating round in Mexico City May 28-29, focusing on rules of origin for industrial goods ahead of July 1 joint review kickoff
Brookfield Asset Management's $1.2B acquisition of World Freight Company from EQT and PAI Partners advancing toward year-end close, bringing infrastructure capital into global air freight at scale
PlusAI running daily commercial autonomous freight on I-35 Laredo-Temple corridor with Ryder using SuperDrive 6.0, targeting full driverless 2027 via TRATON and Hyundai factory-built trucks

Post-Memorial Day freight market normalizing with dry van rates settling $2.47-$2.80/mile after May 23 spike to $3.73/mile, tender rejections softening from 6.25% peak with June Amazon Prime Day surge expected
